title: Seed + triage the candidate-software catalog
context: The project needs a pipeline of candidate tools to contribute to. Without a seeded and triaged catalog, throughput depends on ad-hoc selection and the gate cannot be applied systematically. The catalog must be license-classified (Priority A/B/C) and must flag data-handling concerns (COSMIC/OncoKB, controlled-access data) upfront so contributors can pick tools likely to pass the gate. Depends on gate-002 so the classification rules are locked before the catalog is finalized. riskTier is medium because determining the correct license classification and data-handling disposition for some tools (Priority C, COSMIC/OncoKB-adjacent) requires nuanced licence/de-identification judgment.
objective: Seed the candidate-software catalog with at least 10 entries license-classified as Priority A/B/C, with first-pass triage signals (license, ecosystem, maintenance status, data disposition, COSMIC/OncoKB flag), providing a transparent pipeline of contribution targets.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 10 candidate tools are catalogued with: tool name, ecosystem, license string (marked as indicative — to be re-verified at gate), priority (A/B/C), and a notes column covering the data disposition and any COSMIC/OncoKB or for-profit-vehicle concern.
Priority A tools are clearly OSI-permissive (MIT/BSD/Apache) and work on shipped or synthetic data with minimal data-licensing surface.
Priority B tools are noted as copyleft (GPL/LGPL/AGPL/MPL) or requiring an external open-access dataset to be verified at the gate.
Priority C tools with COSMIC/OncoKB dependencies or open-core concerns are flagged with 'verify/never embed; document-by-reference only'.
The catalog explicitly states that no tool becomes an active contribution task until it passes gate-002 via the triage task (triage-009).
The catalog is committed as a document and is the direct input for triage-009.
resources: SPDX license list; OSI-approved license list
Bioconductor package index; PyPI; CRAN; nf-core pipelines listing
COSMIC license (non-commercial); OncoKB terms of use
output: A committed candidate-software catalog with at least 10 license-classified entries (A/B/C priority), providing a transparent and honestly-triaged pipeline of candidate contribution targets for triage-009 and subsequent milestones.
